candidate_lesson.lesson_text: When internal regime is MOMENTUM but TradingView confirms with 0 signals, the 75% sizing multiplier should be treated as a hard gate against new entries on pairs with green_matches=0, not merely a size reduction.
candidate_lesson.mechanism: The 30-indicator consensus shows conv=100% across all pairs but green_matches=0 — meaning the live signal pattern has NO historical analog in memory. A consensus with no precedent is statistical novelty masquerading as conviction; institutions are not crowding a known setup, so the directional read is untested out-of-sample. USDJPY (hist_hr=0%) and AUDNZD (hist_hr=0%) explicitly have zero historical hit rate, confirming the consensus is firing on configurations that have never paid.
candidate_lesson.testable_rule: if consensus.green_matches == 0 and consensus.hist_hr < 0.50: veto_entry() # require at least one historical green match before trusting 100% conviction
candidate_lesson.expected_impact: Drawdown reduction and Sharpe +0.02-0.04 by filtering precedent-free signals (esp. USDJPY/AUDNZD hist_hr=0%) that would otherwise size up on UNKNOWN regime noise.
candidate_lesson.evidence_from_harvest: All 5 pairs show conv=100% with green_matches=0; USDJPY and AUDNZD show hist_hr=0% despite LONG conviction. Regime is UNKNOWN, TV confirms 0 signals, sizing already cut to 75% — the system is flagging its own unreliability (Tenet 3).

retirement_flag.reason: L8 tightens USDJPY/AUDNZD VIX gate to 13, but current VIX has dropped 18.44→16.78 intraday while USDJPY shows hist_hr=0% — the VIX gate alone is not catching the precedent-free signal failure; the gate may need a green_matches component layered on top.

reflection.system_health_note: System is correctly in a defensive posture — UNKNOWN regime, 0 TV signals, 0 trades in 7 days, and 100% conviction signals with zero historical matches all argue for the current 75% sizing and continued non-execution until precedent reappears.
